steps.task: Create all phase-2 tables with IF NOT EXISTS guards
steps.spec: Migration emits CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for: sleep_logs(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, date date, bedtime timestamptz, wake_time timestamptz, quality smallint, dreams text, hrv_ms int, created_at timestamptz default now()), habits(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, title text, trigger text, cue text, reward text, streak int default 0, active boolean default true, created_at timestamptz default now(), updated_at timestamptz default now()), habit_logs(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, habit_id uuid references habits(id), logged_at timestamptz default now()), gratitude_logs(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, date date, entries jsonb, created_at timestamptz default now()), net_worth_snapshots(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, snapshot_date date, assets_cents bigint, liabilities_cents bigint, created_at timestamptz default now()), future_self_letters(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, content text, deliver_on date, delivered boolean default false, created_at timestamptz default now()), energy_logs(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, logged_at timestamptz, level smallint, notes text, created_at timestamptz default now()), important_dates(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, person_name text, date date, type text, notes text, created_at timestamptz default now()), learning_queue(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, title text, type text, url text, status text default 'queued', key_insight text, created_at timestamptz default now(), updated_at timestamptz default now()). No DROP, no ALTER, no uuid package import.

steps.task: Implement sleep tracking service extending health-intelligence pattern
steps.spec: ESM module. Exports: logSleep(db, userId, payload) inserts into sleep_logs; getSleepSummary(db, userId, days) returns rows; getSleepScoreContribution(db, userId, date) returns +2 if quality>=7 else +1 if quality>=5 else 0 (used by daily scorecard). No top-level browser globals. No AI SDK import; accepts optional callCouncilMember dep for HRV insight narrative. All DB calls use parameterised pg queries.

steps.task: Implement gratitude logging service with AI theme analysis
steps.spec: ESM module. Exports: logGratitude(db, userId, date, entries[3]) upserts into gratitude_logs; getGratitudeThemes(db, callCouncilMember, userId) fetches last 30 days of entries, concatenates, calls callCouncilMember('analyst', prompt) and returns theme summary string; getGratitudeScoreContribution(db, userId, date) returns +1 if a gratitude_logs row exists for that date else 0. No browser globals.

steps.task: Implement future-self letter service with scheduler delivery check
steps.spec: ESM module. Exports: createLetter(db, userId, content, deliverOn) inserts into future_self_letters; checkAndDeliverLetters(db, notifyFn) selects rows where deliver_on <= CURRENT_DATE and delivered=false, calls notifyFn(userId, content) for each, then UPDATE future_self_letters SET delivered=true WHERE id=row.id; getLetters(db, userId) returns all rows for user. notifyFn is injected — no direct import of notification SDK.

steps.task: Implement important-dates service with 7-day and day-of scheduler scan
steps.spec: ESM module. Exports: addDate(db, userId, payload) inserts into important_dates; scanUpcomingDates(db, notifyFn) selects rows where (date - CURRENT_DATE) IN (7,0) using date_part arithmetic ignoring year (anniversary recurrence), calls notifyFn(userId, row) for each match; listDates(db, userId) returns all rows. No browser globals. No uuid import.

steps.task: Scheduler tick for future-self letters, important-dates scans, and calendar protection conflict checks
steps.spec: ESM script. On invocation (called by existing cron runner via import): imports checkAndDeliverLetters from s04, scanUpcomingDates from s05. Calls both with db and a lightweight notifyFn that inserts a row into a `notifications` table (INSERT INTO notifications(user_id,type,payload) VALUES($1,$2,$3) — uses IF NOT EXISTS assumption from existing schema). Also queries energy_logs for each active user to detect if an energy curve needs refresh (calls GET /api/v1/lifeos/energy/curve internally via fetch to localhost — skipped if env SKIP_INTERNAL_FETCH=true). No browser globals. Exports runPhase2Tick(db) as named export.

steps.task: Add all missing phase-3 tables
steps.spec: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for: habits(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, title text, trigger text, cue text, reward text, streak int default 0, active bool default true, created_at timestamptz default now(), updated_at timestamptz default now()); habit_logs(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, habit_id uuid, logged_date date, created_at timestamptz default now()); energy_logs(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, datetime timestamptz, level int check(level between 1 and 10), notes text, created_at timestamptz default now()); nutrition_logs(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, logged_date date, meal_label text, items jsonb, mood_after int, created_at timestamptz default now()); body_logs(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, logged_date date, movement_minutes int, notes text, created_at timestamptz default now()); net_worth_snapshots(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, snapshot_month date, assets numeric, liabilities numeric, net_worth numeric generated always as (assets - liabilities) stored, created_at timestamptz default now()); learning_queue(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, title text, type text, url text, status text default 'queued', key_insight text, created_at timestamptz default now(), updated_at timestamptz default now()); calendar_protection_rules(id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, label text, rrule text, start_time time, end_time time, days_of_week int[], active bool default true, created_at timestamptz default now()); All statements use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS. No DROP or ALTER. Migration is idempotent.

steps.spec: ES-module. Exports: logEnergy(db,userId,datetime,level,notes), getEnergyLogs(db,userId,{from,to}), analyzeEnergyCurve(db,userId,callCouncilMember). analyzeEnergyCurve fetches last 7 days of energy_logs for user, builds hour-by-hour summary string, calls callCouncilMember('analyst', prompt) and returns parsed insight object {peakHours, lowHours, recommendation}. No browser globals. No AI SDK.

steps.task: Implement net-worth snapshot service
steps.spec: ES-module. Exports: upsertSnapshot(db,userId,{snapshot_month,assets,liabilities}), listSnapshots(db,userId), getLatest(db,userId). upsertSnapshot does INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (user_id, snapshot_month) DO UPDATE SET assets=EXCLUDED.assets, liabilities=EXCLUDED.liabilities. snapshot_month is stored as first-of-month date string (YYYY-MM-01). No browser globals. No AI SDK.

steps.task: Implement reading and learning queue service
steps.spec: ES-module. Exports: addItem(db,userId,data), listItems(db,userId,{status}), updateItem(db,userId,id,patch), deleteItem(db,userId,id). patch may include status (queued|reading|done) and key_insight. Updates set updated_at=now(). No browser globals. No AI SDK.

steps.task: Implement calendar-protection rule engine
steps.spec: ES-module. Exports: upsertRule(db,userId,data), listRules(db,userId), deleteRule(db,userId,ruleId), scanConflicts(db,userId,calendarEvents). scanConflicts accepts an array of {start,end} ISO strings representing calendar events, loads active rules for user, and returns an array of conflict objects {event, rule, message} where the event overlaps the protected window on a matching day_of_week. Pure computation after DB load — no AI SDK, no browser globals.

steps.task: Mount all phase-3 API routes and register with auto-register config
steps.spec: ES-module exporting registerLifeosPhase3Routes(app, deps) where deps={db, callCouncilMember}. Mounts: POST/GET /api/v1/lifeos/habits, POST /api/v1/lifeos/habits/:id/log; POST/GET /api/v1/lifeos/energy, GET /api/v1/lifeos/energy/curve; POST/GET /api/v1/lifeos/finance/net-worth; POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/lifeos/learning; POST/GET /api/v1/lifeos/calendar-protection/rules, POST /api/v1/lifeos/calendar-protection/scan. Each handler calls the appropriate service function imported from s2–s6. All routes return JSON. Auth guard: req.user must exist (check deps.requireAuth if provided, else check req.user and return 401). No browser globals. No top-level side-effects — export only. File is self-contained; imports only node builtins and the service files from s2–s6.
steps.note: GAP-FILL: auto-register + rewire imports to real lifeos-* services (builder used nonexistent sN-*-service paths).

steps.task: Add scheduler ticks for cognitive distortion spotting, important-dates proximity check, and calendar-protection conflict scan
steps.spec: ES-module script. Imports pg and the services from s6 (calendar-protection) using relative paths. On a daily tick (setInterval or cron-style loop with 86400000ms): 1) For each user with active calendar_protection_rules, fetch their next 7 days of calendar events from the DB (calendar_events table assumed to exist from prior calendar integration), call scanConflicts, and insert any new conflicts into a notifications queue table (insert IF NOT EXISTS equivalent using ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING keyed on user_id+event_id+rule_id). 2) Log tick completion to stdout. No browser globals. No AI SDK import. Uses DATABASE_URL env var for pg connection. Exports a startScheduler() function as default export and calls it when the module is run directly (import.meta.url check).

steps.task: Digital Twin ui_directives capture service — parse/confirm natural-language layout prefs into flourishing_prefs.ui_directives.
steps.spec: ESM. Export createUiDirectivesService({ pool }) with: parseDirectiveText(text) → { pins?:string[], layout?:string, module_order?:string[], tone?:string } (deterministic keyword/heuristic, no AI import required); proposeDirectives({ userId, text }) returns { proposed, needs_confirm:true }; applyDirectives({ userId, directives }) merges into lifeos_users.flourishing_prefs.ui_directives via jsonb || and returns updated prefs; getDirectives(userId) reads current ui_directives object (default {}). Validate userId. Parameterised SQL only. No browser globals. No edit to server.js. @ssot docs/products/lifeos/PRODUCT_HOME.md + docs/architecture/DIGITAL_TWIN_DOCTRINE.md

steps.task: Twin reaction simulator — predict likely user response to a UI/product idea from twin prefs + optional callCouncilMember.

Export createTwinReactionSimulator({ pool, callCouncilMember }). simulate({ userId, idea, context }) loads flourishing_prefs (+ ui_directives if present), builds a short prediction: { likely_response, confidence: 'low'|'medium', reasons:string[], sources:['prefs'] }. If callCouncilMember provided, may enrich narrative once; on model failure return heuristic-only prediction (never throw). No browser globals. @ssot docs/products/lifeos/PRODUCT_HOME.md

steps.task: Implement LifeOS Perfect Day planning service with DB-backed schedule, check-in, reminders, and daily rating
steps.spec:
This is an ES module service file. Export these named functions:
- export async function planPerfectDay(userId, { wakeTime, schedule }) — upsert a user's perfect-day plan (wake time, ordered activities array with start/end) into a table named `perfect_day` (id, user_id, wake_time, schedule JSONB, created_at, updated_at).
- export async function getPerfectDay(userId) — return the current plan for the user, or null.
- export async function checkIn(userId, { currentActivity, distraction, priority }) — record a check-in and return the next recommended activity or a re-prioritization question.
- export async function getDailyReminders(userId) — return the next 3 upcoming reminders for today based on the plan.
- export async function rateDay(userId, { rating, note, whatMatteredMore }) — upsert a daily rating into `perfect_day_ratings` (id, user_id, perfect_day_id, rating smallint, note, what_mattered_more, created_at).
Use createDbPool from '../services/db.js' to create a local pool: const pool = createDbPool({ validatedDatabaseUrl: process.env.DATABASE_URL, DB_SSL_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED: process.env.DB_SSL_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED });. Use ON CONFLICT for upserts. SQL: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS perfect_day (id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid NOT NULL, wake_time time, schedule jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(), updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(), UNIQUE(user_id)); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS perfect_day_ratings (id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid NOT NULL, perfect_day_id uuid REFERENCES perfect_day(id), rating smallint, note text, what_mattered_more text, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now());

steps.task: Implement LifeOS Perfect Day REST routes with auth and health endpoint
steps.spec:
This is an ES module route file. Export function registerLifeosPerfectDayRoutes(app, deps). Inside the function, create an express.Router and mount it with app.use('/api/v1/lifeos/perfect-day', router). Use deps.requireKey as auth middleware on every endpoint except /health. Endpoints:
- GET /health returns { ok: true } and does NOT require auth.
- POST /plan requires auth, calls planPerfectDay(req.body.user_id, req.body), and returns res.json(result).
- GET /plan requires auth, calls getPerfectDay(req.body.user_id), and returns res.json(result).
- POST /check-in requires auth, calls checkIn(req.body.user_id, req.body), and returns res.json(result).
- GET /reminders requires auth, calls getDailyReminders(req.body.user_id), and returns res.json(result).
- POST /rate requires auth, calls rateDay(req.body.user_id, req.body), and returns res.json(result).
Use import { planPerfectDay, getPerfectDay, checkIn, getDailyReminders, rateDay } from '../services/lifeos-perfect-day.js';. Handle errors with res.status(500).json({ error: error.message }).

steps.task: Register LifeOS Perfect Day route in the auto-register module registry

steps.task: Implement AI photo food logger service with nutrition estimation
steps.spec: ESM service module. Exports: async logFoodWithPhoto(db, userId, { imageUrl, imageBase64, description, loggedAt, callCouncilMember }) and async getFoodLogs(db, userId, { limit=50, since }) and async getNutritionSummary(db, userId, days). Behavior: 1) Validate exactly one of imageUrl or imageBase64 or description is provided. 2) If an image is provided and callCouncilMember is supplied, build a vision prompt describing the food and ask callCouncilMember('vision', prompt) for a JSON string { food, calories, protein_g, carbs_g, fat_g, confidence }. 3) If no image/AI available, accept the user's text description and mark confidence 'user_provided'. 4) Insert into food_logs table (id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, logged_at timestamptz default now(), image_url text, description text, calories int, protein_g int, carbs_g int, fat_g int, confidence text, created_at timestamptz default now()). 5) Return the inserted row. 6) getFoodLogs returns rows ordered by logged_at desc. 7) getNutritionSummary aggregates last N days: SUM(calories, protein_g, carbs_g, fat_g) and COUNT. All DB queries parameterized. No top-level browser globals. No direct import of OpenAI SDK; vision inference injected via callCouncilMember. No fs writes. If callCouncilMember is missing or fails, still log with description and confidence 'parse_failed'.

steps.task: Create builder_runtime_config table for runtime mode switching
steps.spec: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS builder_runtime_config with columns: id text PRIMARY KEY, mode text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'run', updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(), updated_by text; seed with INSERT INTO builder_runtime_config (id, mode, updated_by) VALUES ('builder_runtime_config_singleton', 'run', 'migration') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING. CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS builder_mode_receipts with columns: id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), mode text NOT NULL, triggered_by text, receipt_payload jsonb, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(). All CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS and INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING only. No JS imports. Migration is idempotent.

steps.task: Fix builder_runtime_config.id type from uuid to text and seed sentinel row
steps.spec: Idempotent SQL migration: change builder_runtime_config.id to text, and insert the sentinel row builder_runtime_config_singleton with mode 'run' if missing. Uses information_schema to only alter when not already text. Safe to re-run.

steps.task: Implement runtime mode read/write service backed by builder_runtime_config
steps.spec: Export async functions: getCurrentMode(db) returns {mode,updated_at,updated_by}; setMode(db,mode,triggeredBy,receiptPayload) upserts builder_runtime_config (single-row pattern keyed on a fixed sentinel id or ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) and inserts a builder_mode_receipts row; getReceipts(db,limit) returns recent receipts. Uses only pg pool passed as argument — no top-level imports beyond node builtins. Throws descriptive Error on invalid mode values (allowed: 'run','dry_run','paused').

steps.task: Wire POST /command-center/mode and GET /command-center/mode live using builder-runtime-mode-service
steps.spec: Export registerCommandCenterModeRoutes(app,deps) where deps={db,callCouncilMember}. GET /api/v1/lifeos/command-center/mode returns {mode,updated_at,updated_by}. POST /api/v1/lifeos/command-center/mode accepts {mode,triggered_by} body, validates mode enum (allowed values: 'run', 'dry_run', 'paused', matching builder-runtime-mode-service), calls setMode, returns receipt. All errors return {error:string} with appropriate HTTP status. No top-level browser globals. Import only node builtins and ../services/builder-runtime-mode-service.js.

proof.note: Route was already built in prior commit and is live on the deployed service after the builder_runtime_config id-type fix.

steps.pre_existing: route file existed before this governed run; migration unblocked it

steps.task: Implement phase14 cert service reading phase_ledger from findingsJson
steps.spec: Export async function getPhase14Cert(db) that queries the findings store (SELECT findings_json FROM builder_findings ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1 using CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS builder_findings guard at top via a one-time DDL check) and extracts phase_ledger array from findingsJson. Returns {certified:bool, phase_ledger:[], generated_at:isoString, summary:{total,passed,failed}}. If no findings row exists returns {certified:false,phase_ledger:[],generated_at:now,summary:{total:0,passed:0,failed:0}}. No AI SDK imports; no browser globals.

steps.task: Expose GET /api/v1/builder/cert/phase14 endpoint
steps.spec: Export registerPhase14CertRoutes(app,deps) where deps={db}. GET /api/v1/builder/cert/phase14 calls getPhase14Cert(db) and returns JSON result with Cache-Control no-store. On error returns {error:message} 500. Import only ../services/phase14-cert-service.js. No browser globals.

steps.task: Implement pending Adam query service with priority sort and type badges
steps.spec: Export async function getPendingAdam(db,opts={}) that queries pending_adam table (SELECT id,title,type,priority,status,created_at FROM pending_adam WHERE status NOT IN ('resolved','dismissed') ORDER BY priority ASC, created_at ASC LIMIT coalesce(opts.limit,50)). Export async function resolveAdamItem(db,id,resolvedBy) that UPDATE pending_adam SET status='resolved', resolved_at=now(), resolved_by=$2 WHERE id=$1 RETURNING *. Uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_adam guard. No browser globals. No AI imports.

steps.task: Expose pending Adam panel API endpoints
steps.spec: Export registerPendingAdamRoutes(app,deps) where deps={db}. GET /api/v1/lifeos/command-center/pending-adam returns {items:[],count,fetched_at}. POST /api/v1/lifeos/command-center/pending-adam/:id/resolve accepts {resolved_by} body, calls resolveAdamItem, returns resolved record. Validates id param is uuid-like (36 chars). Returns {error} on failure. Import only ../services/pending-adam-service.js.

steps.task: Register command-center mode and phase14 cert route modules so they mount at boot without editing server.js
steps.spec: Add entries for routes/command-center-mode-routes.js (registerFn:registerCommandCenterModeRoutes) and routes/phase14-cert-routes.js (registerFn:registerPhase14CertRoutes) to the modules array. Preserve all existing entries. Each entry shape: {path, register, enabled, note}. JSON must be valid and minifiable.

steps.task: Build Pending Adam panel UI with priority sort, type badges, one-click resolve
steps.spec: Self-contained dark HTML panel. Polls GET /api/v1/lifeos/command-center/pending-adam every 15s. Renders items as cards with color-coded type badge (research/yellow, decision/red, approval/blue, review/grey), priority number chip, title, created_at relative time. Each card has Resolve button that calls POST .../resolve with {resolved_by:'operator'} then refreshes list. No external CDN — inline CSS only. Mobile-friendly flex layout. No window.fetch polyfill needed (modern browsers). Served as static file.

steps.task: Build Projects Dashboard panel with hover tooltip and click drawer drill-down
steps.spec: Self-contained dark HTML panel. Fetches GET /api/v1/lifeos/command-center/mission-dashboard on load and every 10s. Renders project cards from active_jobs and recent_completed arrays. Hover shows tooltip with {estimate, actual, status}. Click opens slide-in drawer (position:fixed right panel) showing build_plan array, verification_status badge, estimates table. Drawer closes on backdrop click or Esc key. All JS inline. No external CDN. No window globals beyond standard browser API. founder_gated because it is customer-facing UI surface.

steps.task: Build runtime mode switcher UI panel wired to POST /command-center/mode
steps.spec: Self-contained dark HTML panel. On load GETs /api/v1/lifeos/command-center/mode and displays current badge (RUN=green, DRY_RUN=amber, PAUSED=red). Three buttons: Run Now, Dry Run, Pause — each POST {mode:'run'|'dry_run'|'paused', triggered_by:'operator'} then re-fetches status. Shows last receipt payload in collapsible <pre>. Confirms destructive mode changes with window.confirm. Polls every 30s. Inline CSS only dark theme. No external CDN.

steps.task: Update cert script to write phase_ledger into builder_findings table
steps.spec: ESM script. Imports pg from 'pg' (already installed). Reads FINDINGS_JSON_PATH env (default: ./findings.json) with fs/promises readFile. Parses JSON, extracts or derives phase_ledger array (array of {phase,status:'pass'|'fail',note} objects from top-level phases key or constructs from findings array). Upserts one row into builder_findings: INSERT INTO builder_findings(findings_json) VALUES($1) — table created by phase14-cert-service guard so script does not need DDL. Logs {rows_affected, phase_ledger_length} to stdout. Exits 0 on success, 1 on error. No uuid package — DB generates id. Uses DATABASE_URL env for pg connection.

steps.task: Build the chat thought-stream UI module for lifeos-app.html
steps.spec: Classic browser script (NOT an ES module; no top-level import or export statements). Self-initializing on DOMContentLoaded. It must locate the existing chat message container (#lumin-messages) and input/send flow in public/overlay/lifeos-app.html. Intercept fetch calls to the founder chat endpoint (/api/v1/lifeos/builderos/command-control/founder-interface/message) and any legacy /message path; parse the JSON response. For each assistant response, create a thought-stream element above the assistant message bubble. The panel displays concise labels: model routing/provider used, command_ran (yes/no), command_executed, target_file, sha/commit_sha, first_blocker, command_truth, pass_fail, build status, duration_ms. Each label is shown as a collapsible/expandable item with a chevron; default state can be toggled, with a top-level Collapse all / Expand all control. Use existing CSS custom properties from lifeos-theme.js (dark theme, accent, border, text-muted) so it matches the UI. If the response includes a thoughts array (future schema), render those steps verbatim with timestamps. The module must not interfere with sending messages or break if the endpoint changes; if container not found, silently exit. Attach the public API to window.LifeOSChatThoughts only. Do not use top-level `return`. File contains: renderThoughtStream, toggleThought, collapseAllThoughts, expandAllThoughts, thought-stream, command_truth, first_blocker, lumin-messages.

steps.task: Build the chat intent router so the LifeOS chair replies with real workflow content or a real build receipt instead of counsel text
steps.spec: ESM service. Exports classifyFounderIntent(message, context) returning { lane, confidence, payload } where lane is one of counsel, workflow_content, direct_build, ambient, commitment, note, checkin, digital_twin_context. Exports buildSMOSWorkflowReply(intent, context) that returns concrete Social Media OS relocation workflow content (not an apology or essay). Exports buildDirectBuildReply(intent, context, options) that calls POST /api/v1/lifeos/builder/build with the build instruction and x-command-key, waits for the result, and returns a terminal PASS/FAIL receipt with command_truth, pass_fail, command_executed, target_file, commit_sha, duration_ms, and first_blocker. Exports routeByIntent(message, context) that picks the right lane and returns the reply object. No top-level side effects. Use fetch to http://127.0.0.1:$PORT or process.env.PUBLIC_BASE_URL. Handle errors gracefully and return counsel fallback only when no other lane matches.

steps.task: Build the ambient listening overlay for lifeos-app.html
steps.spec: Classic browser script (NOT an ES module; no top-level import/export/require/module.exports; no top-level return). Self-initializing on DOMContentLoaded. Locates the Lumin input area. Adds a microphone toggle button near #lumin-mic-btn. Uses the Web Speech API (SpeechRecognition or webkitSpeechRecognition) with continuous listening when toggled on. On a recognized final transcript, appends it to the Lumin chat input and optionally submits if the user pauses for 2 seconds. Sends interim transcripts to a future POST /api/v1/lifeos/ambient/capture endpoint only if it exists (degrade silently if 404). Displays a visual indicator when listening. Attach public API to window.LifeOSAmbientListener. Do not interfere with normal send. Use existing CSS custom properties.

steps.task: Build the commitment capture and calendar-proposal service
steps.spec: ESM service. Exports captureCommitment(text, { userId, timezone }) that parses natural language like "dentist appointment at 2pm next Tuesday" into a structured commitment object { title, datetime, durationMinutes, timezone, calendarEventRequested }. Inline the natural-language parser; do NOT import any helper files (e.g. './nlp-parser.js', './database.js') because they do not exist. Accept `db` as the FIRST argument on every exported function (pg pool/client) and use ONLY parameterized pg queries (`await db.query('INSERT INTO commitments ... RETURNING *', [userId, title, ...])`). This service does NOT use knex/objection or any ORM. Exports getCommitments(db, userId, opts), resolveCommitment(db, id, { approved, calendarEventId }), and proposeCalendarEvent(commitment) that returns an object with `vevent` (RFC-5545-ish VEVENT string) and `payload` (POST /api/v1/lifeos/calendar/events body). The VEVENT builder must be pure string concatenation. No external API calls by default; calendar write is a future route. No top-level side effects. No imports other than node built-ins.

steps.task: Build the note capture service from natural language
steps.spec: ESM service. Exports captureNote(text, { userId, source, tags }) that extracts the note, suggested tags, and a one-line summary. Exports getNotes(userId, opts), searchNotes(userId, query), and tagNotes(userId, noteIds, tags). Uses a lightweight in-memory or SQL notes table (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS in a separate migration step). Suggested tags can be comma-separated or inferred from keywords. No top-level side effects.

steps.task: Build the daily activity check-in service ("what have you worked on in the last 15 minutes?")
steps.spec: ESM service. Exports startCheckin(db, userId) returning a prompt string by calling getPromptForUser. Exports addCheckinEntry(db, userId, text, { minutesAgo }) that records an activity snippet. Exports getTodaySummary(db, userId) returning a list of entries and a generated summary. Exports getPromptForUser(db, userId) returning the question "Adam, what have you worked on for the last 15 minutes?" plus any context. Exports buildReplyFromEntries(entries) that turns entries into concise bullet notes. Accept `db` as the FIRST argument on every exported function that touches the database (pg pool/client) and use ONLY parameterized pg queries (`await db.query('INSERT INTO checkins ... RETURNING *', [userId, text, ...])`). Do NOT import helper files (e.g. './database-connection.js') because they do not exist. No knex/objection ORM. No top-level side effects. No imports other than node built-ins. The `checkins` table is assumed to exist from a separate migration.

steps.task: Build the normal-language build request router that turns founder instructions into governed BuilderOS tasks
steps.spec: ESM service. Exports parseBuildRequest(text) that extracts a build instruction (e.g. "build a commitment calendar route") and a target file guess. Exports buildBuildPayload(parsed) returning { task, target_file, spec_hint, product_id: "lifeos" }. Exports submitToBuilder(payload, { commandKey, baseUrl }) that POSTs to `${baseUrl}/api/v1/lifeos/builder/build` with `x-command-key: ${commandKey}` and `Content-Type: application/json` using global `fetch`, and returns the JSON receipt. Exports replyFromReceipt(receipt) that turns the build result into a concise founder-facing message. Do NOT import helper files (e.g. './api-client.js') because they do not exist; use global `fetch`. No top-level side effects. No imports other than node built-ins.

steps.task: Build the digital twin context loader for the LifeOS chair
steps.spec: ESM service. Exports getTwinContextForUser(db, userId) that uses ONLY parameterized pg queries (`await db.query('SELECT ...', [userId])`) against existing twin tables (users, twin_simulator, flourishing_preferences, ui_directives, lifeos_notes) and returns a compact summary object { identity, recentNotes, openCommitments, emotionalState, uiDirectives, energyLevel }. Exports injectTwinContext(prompt, userId) that appends the twin summary to the prompt string. Exports updateTwinFromInteraction(db, userId, message, reply) that writes a short learned fact to the twin store with a parameterized pg query. Accept `db` as the FIRST argument on every exported function that touches the database. Do NOT import helper files (e.g. './database.js', './twin-store.js') because they do not exist. No knex/objection ORM. No external API calls. No top-level side effects. No imports other than node built-ins.

steps.task: Build the LifeOS chat action executor that turns intent-router lanes into real execution
steps.spec: ESM service. This file ONLY imports the exact named exports listed below from the three sibling services that already shipped. Sibling API contract (do NOT invent different function names): ./lifeos-commitment-service.js exports captureCommitment(db, text, { userId, timezone }) and getCommitments(db, userId); ./lifeos-note-capture-service.js exports captureNote(text, { userId, source, tags }); ./lifeos-daily-checkin-service.js exports addCheckinEntry(db, userId, text, { minutesAgo }) and getTodaySummary(db, userId). Exports executeCommitment(db, text, { userId, timezone }) that calls `const commitment = await captureCommitment(db, text, { userId, timezone })` and returns a confirmation string including commitment.title and commitment.datetime. Exports executeNote(text, { userId, source, tags }) that calls `const note = captureNote(text, { userId, source, tags })` and returns a confirmation including note.text or note.summary. Exports executeCheckin(db, userId, text, { minutesAgo }) that calls `await addCheckinEntry(db, userId, text, { minutesAgo })`, then `const summary = await getTodaySummary(db, userId)` and returns a bullet list of today's entries. Exports executeBuild(task, { routeToBuilder, operatorKey }) that calls `const receipt = await routeToBuilder(task, operatorKey, { confirmIntent: true })` and returns a plain-English PASS/FAIL message using `receipt.ok`, `receipt.committed`, `receipt.sha`, `receipt.error`, or JSON.stringify(receipt) as fallback. Exports executeAmbient(text) returning a hint to tap the mic icon. Each execute* function MUST wrap in try/catch and return a plain-English failure string, never throw. No top-level side effects. No imports other than the three sibling services and node built-ins.

notes: Adam 2026-07-14: LifeOS Perfect Day added to queue — plan, rate, flexible re-prioritization, ADD-style reminders. The factory must ship s11 (service) then s12 (route) then s13 (auto-register entry). s12 file_contains changed to a stable symbol check; s13 makes the route persist across deploys. | 2026-07-14: Adam — Command Center is not a separate product; its admin surface (builder runtime mode, phase14 cert, pending Adam) is folded into lifeos as lifeos-admin-* steps. The standalone command-center BUILD_QUEUE is deprecated and emptied. | 2026-07-15: lifeos-admin-1b migration deployed and builder_runtime_config.id is now text with sentinel row; lifeos-admin-3 (routes/command-center-mode-routes.js) is live and returning mode=run. GOVERNED_AUTONOMOUS_SHIP=1 is set on the live lumin-web service; the governed loop is expected to resume on the next deploy and continue the remaining BP_PRIORITY product queues. | 2026-07-15: Added Founder Alpha Chat v2 backlog steps lifeos-chat-v2-02 through lifeos-chat-v2-08 to the governed queue: intent router (fixes SENTRY smos_question + drawer_direct_build), ambient listener overlay, commitment→calendar service, note capture, daily check-in, natural build router, and digital-twin context loader. | 2026-07-16: Reordered lifeos-chat-v2-02 through v2-08 to immediately follow lifeos-chat-v2-01 so the Founder Alpha Chat v2 backlog is the next shipped work in the lifeos queue.
